title: OGraph response envelope
description: Structural contract for the media type application/vnd.ograph+json (OGraph specification §6). A response is a result: a single result, a collection result, or an error-only result. Results nest recursively through per-node $edges, each of which is itself a result carrying its own $status/$errors (per-edge partial-failure semantics, §6.4).

result.description: An OGraph result: an outcome-bearing envelope for one operation or one resolved edge.

$status.description: HTTP-aligned status for THIS result scope (§6.4). The root result mirrors the transport response status; a nested edge result carries its own status independent of its parent.

$uri.description: Optional self / re-fetch reference for this result scope. When present on a 2xx result it echoes the value a server would emit in Content-Location, letting a client GET this exact representation without resending the query body (§4.8, §6.5).

$errors.description: Diagnostics scoped to THIS result. Empty or absent on an unqualified success; populated on 4xx/5xx and on partial success where this scope failed while an ancestor succeeded (§6.4).

$count.description: Collection results only: the number of nodes present in THIS page of $nodes (§6.6).

$total.description: Collection results only: the total number of nodes across all pages when the server can compute it; null when the total is unknown or intentionally withheld (§6.6).

$cursor.description: Collection results only: opaque continuation tokens for cursor-mode paging (§6.6). Placeholder members are reserved even while offset paging is the only fully specified mode.

$nodes.description: The payload for this result scope: an object for a single-cardinality result, an array for a collection result, or null when no representation is available (error results, or a failed edge) (§6.2, §6.4).

allOf.$comment: A collection result ($nodes is an array) MUST carry a page count.
$count / $total / $cursor are collection-only paging metadata; they MUST NOT appear on a single-cardinality result whose $nodes is an object.

node.description: A projected node. Non-reserved members are the projected data (§6.2); reserved $-members carry graph metadata. Additional projected members are permitted.

$uri.description: Optional stable identity/self reference for this individual node.

edges.description: Resolved edges keyed by edge name (§6.3). Each value is a nested result with its own status and errors (§6.4).

cursor.description: Cursor-mode paging continuation tokens (§6.6). Reserved for the cursor paging mode; offset paging leaves these null.

next.description: Opaque token that, when supplied to the same operation, yields the following page; null when this is the last page.

prev.description: Opaque token that yields the preceding page; null when this is the first page.

error.description: One diagnostic. code/message are required; the remaining members refine location and nesting. Query-syntax diagnostics reuse the OGraph G-code vocabulary (§6.4).

code.description: Stable machine-readable identifier (e.g. a G-code such as "G0002", or a semantic/policy code).

severity.description: Diagnostic severity; defaults to "error" when omitted.

target.description: The offending member, keyword, or clause (a query path or JSON pointer).

position.description: Zero-based source position within the query text, for editor and syntax diagnostics.

details.description: Nested diagnostics that elaborate on this one.
